globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.

mandatoryDirectives.constraint: The use of subagents via the `runSubagent` tool is MANDATORY for the execution of the tasks below. Tasks are grouped specifically to be run in distinct subagent contexts to prevent context window exhaustion. Do not attempt to solve all groups in a single turn.

statusSection.currentState: T001-T076 completed (coverage audits, API optimization, profiling, security, bug fixes, features). T077 (Windows) In Progress — code changes committed (307165b), actual Windows test run pending. T078-T102 planned: platform verification, documentation overhaul, CHANGELOG, shell completion, QoL features, session cleanup, dependency audit, final integration, scope expansion.

sequentialTasks.task: T124: Code review chunking for large diffs
sequentialTasks.description: Implement automatic splitting of large diffs into LLM-digestible chunks in code-review command. Configurable token/line budget per chunk. Series of prompts (chunk 1/N...) with shared context header.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Already fully implemented. diff_splitter.go with SplitDiff (500-line default, bin-packing, hunk-level splitting). review-chunks JS command in code_review_script.js. 13 tests across diff_splitter_test.go and code_review_chunk_test.go.

sequentialTasks.task: T125: Add osm:json module for structured JSON manipulation
sequentialTasks.description: Go-backed JSON utilities: jsonpath queries, json merge-patch (RFC 7386), json diff, json schema validation. For data transformation workflows.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Created osm:json module (jsonmod pkg) with 7 functions: parse, stringify, query (dot/bracket/wildcard), mergePatch (RFC 7386), diff (JSON Pointer), flatten, unflatten. 80+ tests. Registered in register.go. Docs updated.

sequentialTasks.task: T126: Config directory migration to ~/.osm and discovery path consolidation
sequentialTasks.description: Migrate config directory from current location to ~/.osm (per docs/todo.md). Consolidate osm configuration to align with https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings model. Change script and goal discovery behavior: aggressively cull/tidy functionality, STRICTLY support discovery within project-level directories (up-the-CWD discovery that terminates at the nearest parent) and in user/system-level directories. Review current implementation for user and system discovery paths.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Migrated ~/.one-shot-man→~/.osm (config), one-shot-man/sessions→osm/sessions (storage), .one-shot-man/sync→.osm/sync (sync). GetConfigPath() with 4-level fallback: env→new dir→legacy file→new default. 6 new tests. 26 files changed, 212 insertions. Committed 58094c9.

sequentialTasks.task: T127: Add 'which one is better' builtin goal
sequentialTasks.description: Add 'which one is better' builtin goal to internal/command/goal_builtin.go. Implement exhaustive options analysis, tailored for a wide range of use cases. Create specific use case variants, leveraging existing patterns (stateVars, hotSnippets, flagDefs, promptOptions) like morale-improver, bug-buster, code-optimizer. Target maximizing utility and general usefulness. MUST be integration tested properly.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Added which-one-is-better goal (14→15 builtins, 13→14 categories). 5 comparison types (general/technology/architecture/strategy/design) via comparisonTypeInstructions PromptOptions. set-type command. 2 hot-snippets (deeper-analysis, devils-advocate). PostCopyHint. 6-phase analysis methodology. 8 new tests + 1 updated test. Docs: goal.md, architecture.md, CHANGELOG.md, todo.md.

sequentialTasks.task: T128: Replace git shell-outs in sync.go with go-git/v6 (clone, add, commit, push, pull)
sequentialTasks.description: Replace exec.Command git calls in internal/command/sync.go with github.com/go-git/go-git/v6 library. Operations to convert: clone, add, commit, push, pull. Design constraints: prefer streaming file reads/writes directly from git objects (avoid temp files, avoid buffering entire files to disk), go-git API is 'fucky' - use pragmatically where it works, don't force it where it fights you. Preserve: pull uses --rebase strategy, push commits with timestamp message 'osm sync: <RFC3339>', conflict detection ('CONFLICT' or 'could not apply'). Exception: diff commands (osm ctx add --from-diff) continue using CLI git - no change. Update sync_test.go to work with go-git or mock appropriately.

sequentialTasks.task: T129: Replace git shell-outs in sync_startup.go with go-git/v6 (auto-pull)
sequentialTasks.description: Replace exec.Command git call in internal/command/sync_startup.go with go-git. SyncAutoPull function runs git pull --rebase on startup when sync.auto-pull=true. Preserve non-blocking behavior, error handling to stderr. Use same go-git patterns as sync.go. Update sync_startup_test.go accordingly.

sequentialTasks.task: T130: Verify sync tests pass after go-git migration
sequentialTasks.description: Run full sync test suite (internal/command/sync_test.go, internal/command/sync_startup_test.go) to verify all behaviors preserved: save/list/load, init/push/pull, auto-pull, path injection. Ensure no regressions. Coverage should remain high.

Run TestSuperDocument_BacktabNavigation multiple times in parallel (e.g., 5-10 iterations) and on Linux via Docker to verify stability. ANY flakiness MUST be fixed immediately. VERIFY FIXES by re-running the same parallel/Linux tests until NO flakes occur for minimum 5 consecutive runs. Document any changes made and confirm stability.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Root cause: inconsistent inter-keystroke delays (4-20ms) across PTY integration tests caused character drops under CPU load. Fix: added ptyCharDelay constant (25ms) and replaced all 13 character-typing delays across both test files. 5/5 consecutive passes verified.

sequentialTasks.task: T131: Remove unused sync.enabled config key
sequentialTasks.description: Remove sync.enabled from internal/config/schema.go (line 495) - it's defined but never used anywhere in the codebase. Also update any tests or documentation referencing it.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Removed sync.enabled from schema.go, schema_test.go, benchmark_test.go, config.md, configuration.md, git-sync-design.md. CHANGELOG entry added. todo.md annotated. Also fixed pre-existing completion test missing which-one-is-better goal.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make all' with full logging after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute 'make make-all-with-log' after: (a) Each code change, (b) Each test addition, (c) Each task completion, (d) Each bug fix.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: You will ALWAYS aim for 100% effective test coverage, and VERIFY test coverage by means of the tooling available to you. You will NOT proceed to the next task until you have achieved 100% effective test coverage for the current task, and have verified that coverage with the tools available to you.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.
